title: Patch the SQL Injection Login Bypass
objective: Patch the admin login endpoint so it can no longer be bypassed via SQL injection.
briefing: The auth logs show a successful admin login that came from a malformed query, not a real password. The login endpoint is building SQL by pasting user input directly into the query string.
hints: Check the auth logs closely - does one of those logins look like it used SQL syntax instead of a password?
The fix isn't restarting a service - it's changing how the query is built.
debrief: The endpoint was building SQL like: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '<input>' AND password = '...' - pasting user input straight into the query. Parameterized queries (a.k.a. prepared statements) fix this by sending the user's input to the database as data, in a separate channel from the SQL command itself, so characters like ' and -- can never change the query's structure no matter what's typed. This is the standard fix for Injection (OWASP A03:2021): never build a query by string-concatenating untrusted input - always use parameterized queries or an ORM that does so under the hood.

logs: [AUTH] admin login from {ip} - bypassed via malformed query

cases.output:
patched /var/www/admin/login.php
query builder now uses bound parameters... done

logs_append: [FIX] login.php now uses parameterized queries - injection no longer possible
